summary: Iteration 3 — Validated the new email-OTP signup gate (Resend mock mode). Added 7 new pytest cases under TestEmailOTP covering /auth/config flag, mock send returning test_otp=123456, 60s cooldown 429, wrong/no-send 400 verify errors, success-then-register issuing JWT with verified/email_verified=true, register-without-verify rejection (400 'verify your email first'), and duplicate-email rejection. Updated session-scoped customer fixture and two pre-existing stranger-registration tests to call /auth/email/send + /auth/email/verify before /auth/register (previously failing after the new gate). Final pytest result: 43 passed, 0 failed in ~2.7s. Frontend signup 4-step flow on /signup verified end-to-end on the public preview URL: role=customer → details → 'Continue → Verify Email' → step 3 shows 'Test code: 123456' in the body AND a bottom-right toast 'Test code: 123456' → entered 123456 → 'Verify & Continue' → step 4 'Almost there!' shows '✓ Email Verified' card with email + role → 'Create My Account ✨' → landed on /customer with 'Welcome, UITest' and 'Email verified ✓' toast. Regression: all prior backend flows (login/admin, artist seeded, booking pricing, payment init/verify mock, contract accept→PDF, invoice PDF, refund admin-only, webhook disabled) still pass.

minor.issue: Cooldown enforced even after a successful verify — if a user re-opens signup for the same email within 60s, /auth/email/send returns 429. Frontend 'Resend Code' is also 60s gated; that's acceptable but slightly user-hostile right after a fresh verify.

Mock-mode 'sent_at' uses utcnow() helper which returns an isoformat string while datetime.fromisoformat parses it back — works, but if utcnow() ever returns a tz-naive ISO the timedelta math will throw and fall back to 'now' (effectively no cooldown). Defensive but worth pinning to timezone.utc consistently.
Carry-over: SMS OTP endpoints still exist even though email is the primary verification flow now. Recommend removing or hiding from public docs to avoid confusion / bypass risk.

ui_bugs.issue: Carry-over (iter 1+2): still triggers React hydration warning in console on every artist page.
Carry-over: <title> remains 'Emergent | Fullstack App' instead of 'BookTalent'.

issues: The 'Test code: 123456' hint is shown twice — once inline under the heading and again as a persistent bottom-right toast. Slightly redundant; can be hidden once user types into the OTP field.

action_items: (Optional) Remove or deprecate /auth/otp/send + /auth/otp/verify SMS endpoints since email is the new verification path.
Carry-over: fix <option><span> hydration warning in ArtistProfile.jsx.
Consider de-duplicating the 'Test code' hint on /signup step 3 (currently shown inline AND as a toast).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2100+ lines — auth + email-OTP + payments + admin + contracts in a single module. Strongly recommend splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/email.py, routers/payments.py, etc.

email_service.generate_otp() returns the deterministic literal '123456' in mock mode — fine for testing, but a thin layer of guard (env flag) would prevent any chance of this leaking into production should keys be misconfigured.

/auth/email/send stores the raw OTP in MongoDB; for production safety, hash the OTP (e.g., bcrypt or HMAC-sha256 with JWT_SECRET) and compare hashes on verify so a DB leak doesn't reveal active codes.
/auth/email/verify does not throttle wrong attempts — a single record can be brute-forced 1M attempts within the 10-minute window. Add a max_attempts (e.g., 5) + lock-out, similar to login lockout.

/auth/register pulls email_otps record and trusts verified flag but never deletes/invalidates it after register, so the same verified record could be reused if the user is later deleted and re-registered. Suggest deleting/marking 'consumed' on successful register.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added TestEmailOTP with 7 tests, added _verify_email() helper, updated customer fixture and 2 stranger-registration tests to do email-verify before register)
test_credentials: Admin admin@booktalent.com/Admin@123, Customer customer@booktalent.com/Customer@123, Artist priya@booktalent.com/Artist@123, Email OTP (mock) 123456, Payment OTP (mock) 123456. RESEND_API_KEY empty → mock mode (verified via GET /api/auth/config → {email_provider_enabled:false}).
seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_email_*, TEST_cool_*, TEST_wrong_*, TEST_nosend_*, TEST_reg_*, TEST_block_*, TEST_ui_* records created in db.email_otps and db.users during this run. One UI signup left behind a customer user 'UITest User' (TEST_ui_<ts>@booktalent.com) on /customer dashboard. Safe to purge with email regex ^TEST_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email OTP runs in MOCK MODE (RESEND_API_KEY empty in /app/backend/.env). /auth/email/send returns {sent:true, mock:true, test_otp:'123456'}. /auth/email/verify accepts '123456'. /auth/register requires a prior verified email_otps record. SMS phone OTP endpoints (/auth/otp/send|verify) still exist but are no longer the primary signup path. When live Resend keys are added later, retest: /auth/config → email_provider_enabled:true; /auth/email/send should NOT return test_otp in response and an actual email should be sent via Resend; generate_otp() should yield a random 6-digit code (not 123456). Frontend signup testids: role-customer/artist/agency/corporate, signup-next-1, signup-first-name, signup-last-name, signup-email, signup-phone, signup-password, signup-confirm, signup-send-otp, signup-email-otp, signup-verify-otp, signup-resend-otp, signup-submit. Mock 'Test code: 123456' surfaces both inline on step 3 and as a corner toast.
rca of the issue: No critical bugs found. Two pre-existing tests (test_contract_pdf_forbidden_for_stranger, test_invoice_pdf_forbidden) failed on first run because they POST /auth/register for stranger accounts without going through the new email-verify gate. Root cause: new requirement in /auth/register checks db.email_otps for a verified record. Fix applied: added _verify_email() helper and called it before each stranger-register in tests. All 43 tests now pass.
